threat: Compromised model attempts direct Android authority
controls: Isolated UID; no service-manager/permission/storage access; Broker-only proposal path

threat: Generic execution primitive enables arbitrary Android action
controls: Closed schemas; prohibited primitive review; no fallback

threat: Broker confused deputy accepts forged UID/user/package/attribution
controls: Derive Binder/framework identity; bind context hash

threat: Provider launders its permissions for unauthorized caller
controls: Bound authorization; caller/audience checks; callee enforcement

threat: Cross-user/profile state or token reuse
controls: Per-user Registry/grants/queues/stores/epochs; lifecycle teardown

threat: Spoofed or detached confirmation/authentication
controls: Trusted UI; exact challenge hash; auth reference; nonce/expiry

threat: Broker/provider directly accesses HAL, device, native socket or kernel
controls: No code dependency; exact SELinux denial; manager-only future path

threat: Sensitive observation/knowledge data overcollection or retention
controls: Schema minimization, redaction, rate/budget/retention, metadata-only audit

threat: Missing/dead/stale dependency triggers broader fallback
controls: Explicit availability; no fallback; generation/health expiry

threat: Effect occurs without durable evidence
controls: Audit reservation bound into authorization before dispatch

threat: Parser/model/provider code compromises system_server
controls: Minimal Authority only; untrusted parsing/work out of process

threat: Broker compromise reaches broad Android services
controls: Unique UID/domain; named endpoints; no generic manager/service access

threat: Provider family compromise exposes unrelated privileges/data
controls: Unique family UID/domain/storage and permission set

threat: Malicious extension loads code into trusted process
controls: Quarantine parser; sealed metadata; no class/native loading; Authority admission only

threat: Extension self-assigns low risk, trust or permission
controls: Platform policy overlay; extension output candidate only

threat: Package replacement/signature downgrade leaves stale active binding
controls: Revoke old binding first; exact lineage/version/hash; new generation

threat: Platform signature or shared UID becomes blanket trust
controls: Unique UID; dedicated signature permissions; Registry+SELinux checks

threat: Provider/model lowers OS-owned risk classification
controls: Final platform catalog/decision; advice can only restrict

threat: Critical action auto-retries after ambiguous outcome
controls: Single-use token; no auto-retry; reconcile Audit/provider under fresh policy

threat: Observation/event payload is treated as authorization
controls: Lease never grants execution; fresh request/policy required; token forbidden

threat: Registry mixed-generation race resolves inconsistent provider/policy
controls: Atomic immutable snapshots; one generation per decision; stale restart before auth only

threat: Authorization replay/mutation/cross-provider use
controls: Hash/audience/user/purpose/epoch/nonce/expiry binding; single-use state

threat: Audit ledger tampering, truncation or silent reset
controls: Single writer; hash chain; journal; corruption isolation; append-only corrections

threat: Audit stores credentials, secrets, raw tokens or sensitive payload
controls: Pre-persistence redaction/forbidden fields; metadata/digests only

threat: Observation flood exhausts memory or blocks system threads
controls: Bounded queue/rate/backpressure/dedupe; dedicated process; drop/gap semantics

threat: Extension manifest causes parser OOM/hang/traversal/remote fetch
controls: Bounded closed schema; isolated process; no remote/path/code; timeout/resource budget

threat: UI/Settings directly executes or mutates policy/storage
controls: Authority-mediated operations; no provider/raw store access

threat: Runtime component crash leaves stale authority or hidden work
controls: Death monitoring; epoch/revocation; reattest/reconcile/new generation

threat: Power loss creates inconsistent effect/audit state
controls: Durable reservation/journal; phase recovery; unknown outcome explicit

threat: APEX/package downgrade reinterprets newer security state permissively
controls: Compatibility handshake; quarantine; compatible reader or disabled state

threat: Emergency disable fails to revoke in-flight/cached state
controls: Epoch increment; stop admission; revoke tokens/leases; drain/checkpoint

threat: Test no-op provider or mode ships in production
controls: Separate test target/config; production inventory release gate

threat: Real capability provider enters P0 through transitive dependency
controls: Target visibility; P0 aggregate exclusion; installed inventory

threat: Unreviewed source path modifies existing Android semantics
controls: Exact path/first-write map; baseline/status verification; ACP required

threat: Pre-existing reconstruction changes are overwritten or blamed on ProdX
controls: Capture dirty state/heads; preserve; compare allowed delta only

threat: Normative reference drifts without impact analysis
controls: Content hashes; immutable copies; ACP; new version/baseline; traceability regeneration

threat: Requirement/test loses owner or is falsely marked complete
controls: Stable owner IDs; gate tracker; independent evidence review

threat: Logging/dumps/error reveals secrets or policy internals
controls: Typed safe errors; redacted diagnostics; field-level data policy

threat: Resource exhaustion in system_server from validation/event/provider work
controls: Bounded small Authority; parsing/queues/work out of process; quotas/timeouts

threat: Android cannot boot when ProdX module/service is absent or broken
controls: Feature-gated dormant anchor; optional bindings; watchdog rollback; disabled default
